title: Add SQLite embedding cache with content hashing to skip redundant computation
description: Add a hash-based embedding cache to memory.db so that bge-small-en-v1.5 embeddings are computed once per unique text and reused on subsequent runs. Currently, every pipeline run recomputes embeddings for all preflight items during dedup (preflight/run_all.py _dedup_batch) and for findings storage. With ~400 items per run and 10-day lookback, many texts are re-embedded across days. A SHA256 content hash keyed cache in SQLite would eliminate redundant computation, reducing embedding time by ~60% on warm runs. The 384-dim float32 vectors are only ~1.5KB each, so even 100K cached entries would be under 150MB. WAL mode already enabled on memory.db ensures concurrent reads during parallel agent dispatch.
requirements: Add embedding_cache table to memory.db schema (content_hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY, embedding BLOB, model_name TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Add cached_embed_text() and cached_embed_texts() functions to memory/embeddings.py that check cache before computing
Hash input text with SHA256 before cache lookup
Update preflight/run_all.py _dedup_batch() to use cached embedding functions
Update memory/findings.py store_finding() to use cached embeddings when storing new findings
Add cache hit/miss metrics logged at INFO level per batch
Include model_name in cache key so model upgrades invalidate old entries
done_criteria: Second consecutive pipeline run completes embedding phase at least 50% faster than first run
Cache hit rate logged per preflight dedup batch
All existing tests pass (python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q)
New tests verify cache hit returns identical vector to fresh computation
New test verifies model_name mismatch triggers recomputation

design_spec.architecture_impact: Adds one table to memory.db. No schema changes to existing tables. Embedding functions gain an optional db parameter for cache access.

context: Pattern from https://logarithmicspirals.com/blog/sqlite-openai-embeddings-astro-builds/ and https://medium.com/@tfmv/sqlite-for-embeddings-3fe30ee5efbf — SQLite hash-based embedding caches cut recomputation by 60%+ in production. MindPattern already uses fastembed with ONNX runtime (memory/embeddings.py) and SQLite WAL mode (memory/db.py), so adding a cache table is a natural extension.
